title: Dietitian

rationales.taskAutomation: Meal-plan generation, nutrient analysis and standard dietary-tracking are substantially automatable; personalized counseling and behavior-change work are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: Consumer and clinical nutrition-AI apps that generate meal plans and analyze intake are common and improving quickly.
rationales.adoption: AI nutrition apps are widely used by consumers and increasingly integrated into clinical dietetics workflows.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can generate meal plans and track macros end to end, leaving the dietitian to handle counseling, adherence and medical nutrition therapy.

tasksAtRisk: Standard dietary intake tracking and reporting
tasksGrowing: Medical nutrition therapy for complex conditions
Clinical collaboration on complex care plans
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 29-1031 (Dietitians and Nutritionists), p25-p75 rounded
insight: Consumer apps now generate meal plans and track macros about as well as a routine consult would, pushing dietitians toward medical nutrition therapy and behavior-change counseling for complex conditions. The generic meal-planning end of the field is the most exposed.
